mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises during recovery phases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance with Rank IC below 0.0 in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and wage growth, which directly supports aggregate demand and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in greater than 50% of months where PAYEMS increases, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, it forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and erodes real corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of triggered months, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly with a p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and declining unemployment confirms a broad-based economic expansion, which validates equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 60-day returns in greater than 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side constraints and reduced economic velocity, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances when INDPRO falls during tightening periods, or the regime-specific predictive power vanishes with Rank IC below 0.01.
